description_long: This is a small, military-style airplane with a delta wing configuration and a camouflage paint scheme, featuring a prominent number '55' in red on the vertical stabilizer. The wings are broad and sharply swept, giving the aircraft a triangular appearance from above. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a single nose wheel, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is positioned forward on the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a distinct canopy section. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and no visible horizontal stabilizers, consistent with a delta wing design. The surface finish is matte, and the materials suggest a combination of metal and composite construction.
description: A small, camouflaged delta-wing military airplane with fixed landing gear and a forward, enclosed cockpit. The number '55' is marked in red on the tail fin.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the triangular delta wing shape and camouflage pattern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the red '55' on the tail and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wide delta wingspan, nose wheel, and main landing gear.
description_view_3: Right side view with the tail number and cockpit canopy visible, mirroring the left side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Delta wing, low-wing configuration with sharply swept leading edges and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engine; engine placement not apparent from exterior.
attribute_tail_design: Single vertical stabilizer with no separate horizontal stabilizers, consistent with a delta wing tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and one nose wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy section.
